spicalTrip.describe:
Saving the ship Assina
After a continuous operation lasting more than 17 days, in extremely challenging marine conditions with strong currents, high water pressure, and almost zero visibility, a Sudanese team from Red Sea Secrets, in collaboration with Blue Shark Diving, achieved an exceptional feat. They succeeded in rescuing the #ASINA_CARGO ship, which had completely sunk to a depth of over 16 meters in the Red Sea, in an operation described as one of the most precise and professional marine missions 🏊🥽🦺. The operation proved beyond doubt that Sudanese professionals 💪 are capable of carrying out the most complex marine rescue operations with efficiency that matches international standards. This success was not just a ship rescue, but a message of pride in Sudan's maritime capabilities. The national teams proved that professionalism is not exclusive to outsiders, and that the people of this country are capable of bringing life back to sunken iron in the depths of the Red Sea through willpower, knowledge, and faith in their abilities.

shipSupply.name: gido madany
shipSupply.supplyLitter:
Faja Island
Faja Island 🏝️ Location: Located near Port Sudan, it is one of the small islands scattered along the coast. Access is usually by small boats from the port or nearby marinas. Nature: The island is surrounded by crystal-clear waters and diverse coral reefs, making it a popular destination for diving and snorkeling enthusiasts. Marine Life: Home to a wide variety of colorful fish and coral reefs, and occasionally dolphins and sea turtles can be spotted in its waters. Activities: Ideal for short trips, simple camping, diving, and swimming. There is little infrastructure, so the place remains close to its natural wilderness. Climate: Shares the same coastal climate (hot and dry most of the year), but the sea breeze makes the weather more pleasant, especially in the evenings. 🔹 In short, Faja Island is a perfect spot for marine nature lovers, especially those who enjoy camping and diving.
